content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission.
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

October 21, 1976


Ms. Zelna Lee Morrow
City Clerk, Williams
Post Office Box 125
Williams, California 95087

Re: Conflict of Interest Code for the Williams City Council

Dear Ms. Morrow:

In response to your letter of October 5, 1976, in which
you ask for an explanation of the meaning of the last sentence
in Section 4, Circumstances Requiring Disqualification, which
states:

No member shall be prevented from making
or participating in the making of any deci-
sion to the extent his or her participation
is legally required for the decision to be
made.

The inclusion of this sentence in Section 4 paraphrases
the meaning of Government Code §87101, which provides:

Section 87100 does not prevent any public
official from making or participating in
the making of a governmental decision to the
extent his participation is legally required
for the decision to be made. The fact that
an official's vote is needed to break a tie
does not make his participation legally re-
quired for purposes of this section.

A further clarification of the meaning of "legally
required participation" is contained in the Fair Political
Practices Commission's regulation in 2 Cal. Adm. Code
§18701, which states as follows:


TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE (LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322.6441

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only a few very faint marks visible at the top of the page that are too unclear to transcribe with certainty.

Ms. Zelna Lee Morrow
October 21, 1976                                          Page Two


          (a)  A public official is not legally
     required to make or to participate in the
     making of a governmental decision within
     the meaning of Government Code Section
     87101 unless there exists no alternative
     source of decision consistent with the
     purposes and terms of the statute auth-
     orizing the decision...


          Suppose, for example, that a City Council finds that
     because of personal conflicts, three members of a five-member
     Council are disqualified from acting upon a decision, and
     that the decision is such that no other ruling body can act
     upon that decision.  This situation would then fall within
     the exception as stated in 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18701, and the
     participation of the three disqualified Council persons would
     be deemed legally required.  Further, 2 Cal. Adm. Code
     §18701 sets forth a procedure which must be followed in
     the event that such a situation as described above should
     occur.  The Regulation provides:

          (b)  Whenever a public official who has
     a financial interest in a decision is le-
     gally required to make or to participate
     in making such a decision, he or she shall:

               (1)  Disclose as a matter of official
          public record the existence of the fin-
          ancial interest;

               (2)  Describe with particularity the
          nature of the financial interest before
          he or she makes or participates in mak-
          ing the decision;

               (3)  Attempt in no way to use his or
          her official position to influence any
          other public official with respect to
          the matter;

               (4)  State the reason there is no
          alternative source of decision-making
          authority;

               (5)  Participate in making the decision
          only to the extent that such participation
          is legally required.

          (c)  This regulation shall be construed
     narrowly, and shall not be construed to
     permit voting to break a tie.

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white with only very faint marks visible at the top. I cannot reliably transcribe any clear text from this document image.

Ms. Zelna Lee Morrow
October 21, 1976                                        Page Three


     Thank you for your inquiry.  If I can be of further
assistance, please call me at 916/322-6444, Conflicts of
Interest Division.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Alfredo Flores
                                        Staff Attorney
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                          Division


AF:gb

The image appears to be mostly blank or white with only a few small marks or artifacts visible at the top (what appear to be small curved lines or marks on the left and center-top portions, and some dots on the right side). There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe in this document image.

City of Williams
CITY HALL
WILLIAMS, CALIFORNIA 95987

October 5, 1976

Fair Political Practice Board
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA

Attention: Delbert L. Spurloch, Jr.

Dear Sir:

At the City Council meeting of October 4, 1976,
a resolution adopting a conflict of interest
code application to the members of the City
Council was presented for their consideration.
The resolution followed exactly the example prepared by your staff. Council would like to know
the meaning & reasoning for the last sentence in
section 4 which reads: "No member shall be prevented from making or participating in the making
of any decision to the extent his or her participation is legally required for the decision
to be made."

Your early reply to this matter will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

Zelna Lee Morrow
City Clerk

[The image appears to be almost entirely blank/white with only some very faint marks at the top and a small mark or spot in the bottom left corner. The content is not legible enough to transcribe with confidence.]

The inclusion of this sentence in Section 4 paraphrases the meaning of Government Code §87101, which provides: Section 87100 does not prevent any public official from making or participating in the making of a governmental decision to the extent his participation is legally required for the decision to be made. The fact that an official's vote is needed to break a tie does not make his participation legally required for purposes of this section.
